  Support the Mission
  ↓






  
    OUR MISSION
    AI education should befree and open to everyone.
    Everyone calls AI the new electricity — but electricity is useless without engineers who can build the grid. For AI to be efficient, reliable, and safe, the world needs engineers who understand how to build it.
    That knowledge should be accessible to anyone willing to learn. This curriculum is our commitment to making it so.
  

  
    
      
      
    
    Live readership — 180+ countries
  

  
    23,000+ stars · 243,000+ readers · 180+ countries

    
      Our goal: 1,000,000 AI engineers by 2030
      Next milestone: 100,000 ★ — we're at 23,000+.Every star helps the next student discover this resource.

title: Community
text:
Machine Learning Systems is taught at 40+ universities across six continents — from Bogota to Johannesburg, from Kobe to Nairobi.
  Every chapter and lab is open and free — and we ship hardware kits to partner universities that need them.
  AI has no borders. Neither should the education to build it.

title: Community
text:
Beyond the Textbook
Every chapter is free to read — but access means more than a URL. It means hardware, training, and showing up in person.

  
    
    Global Academic Network
    40+ universities across six continents teaching ML systems — from Harvard and Columbia to institutions across Africa, Asia, and Latin America. Through our partnership with ICTP in Trieste, we provide curriculum, instructor training, and ongoing support.
  
  
    
    Hardware Where It's Needed
    We ship real hardware — Arduino and Seeed edge devices — to universities that couldn't otherwise afford them. Every partner institution receives kits so students can learn on real silicon, not just simulations.
  
  
    
    Workshops on the Ground
    The annual SciTinyML workshop series at ICTP in Trieste brings researchers and students together to apply ML to real scientific challenges — from environmental monitoring to health diagnostics.
  
  
    
    Free and Open — Always
    The full two-volume textbook is available as free HTML, PDF, and EPUB. The HarvardX TinyML Professional Certificate on edX reaches thousands of learners worldwide. CC-BY-NC-SA licensed so anyone can teach from it.

title: About
text:
ABOUT
  
  The story of an open-source curriculum that grew into a global movement for AI engineering education.

title: About
text:
Our Story

In 2020, a new graduate course launched at Harvard: CS 249r, focused on the emerging field of TinyML — machine learning on the smallest, most constrained devices. There was no textbook for it. So we started writing one.
The first version was rough — course notes stitched into chapters, shared with students as a PDF. But when we open-sourced it on GitHub, something unexpected happened. Students and educators around the world started reading it, contributing to it, and teaching from it.
In 2021, the International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP) in Trieste partnered with us to form the TinyML4D Academic Network — 40+ universities across six continents, each receiving curriculum materials and hardware kits. What started as a Harvard course was now taught in Bogotá, Nairobi, Kobe, and Johannesburg.
By 2024, the material had outgrown TinyML. Students were asking about training at scale, distributed systems, fleet orchestration — the full systems stack. The textbook expanded into two volumes: Introduction to Machine Learning Systems and Machine Learning Systems at Scale. The website became mlsysbook.ai. MIT Press signed on for a hardcover edition.
Today the project is more than a textbook. It is a full curriculum — with interactive labs, a build-from-scratch ML framework (TinyTorch), lecture slides, hardware kits, instructor resources, and an interview prep guide. All of it open. All of it free.

title: About
text:
Mission

The world is rushing to build AI systems. It is not engineering them.
Machine learning has transformed every industry, but the education pipeline has not kept pace. Students learn algorithms in isolation, divorced from the silicon that executes them, the memory hierarchies that constrain them, and the networks that distribute them. The result: a generation of practitioners who can call model.fit() but cannot reason about why their model is slow, where the bottleneck lives, or how to fix it without guessing.
Machine Learning Systems exists to close that gap. We teach AI as infrastructure — governed by physical laws, shaped by engineering constraints, and measurable through quantitative reasoning. Our thesis is simple: constraints drive architecture. You do not choose a Transformer because it is trendy; you choose it because of how it parallelizes on real silicon.

  
    ⚙️
    Principles Over APIs
    We teach the physics beneath the framework. Memory hierarchies, not torch.cuda(). Arithmetic intensity, not library calls. Knowledge that endures beyond any single tool.
  
  
    📐
    Quantitative Reasoning
    Every claim is backed by a number. Students learn to estimate, measure, and verify — the same skills that separate a systems engineer from a script runner.
  
  
    🌍
    Open and Global
    Free HTML, PDF, and EPUB. Used at universities across six continents. Licensed under CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0 so anyone can teach from it, adapt it, and build on it.
  
  
    🔬
    Full-Stack Curriculum
    Not just a textbook. TinyTorch, hardware kits, interactive labs, lecture slides, and instructor resources — everything needed to teach a two-semester course.
  



  
    22,800+
    GitHub Stars
  
  
    2
    Volumes
  
  
    32
    Chapters
  
  
    95+
    Contributors
  
  
    6
    Continents

title: About
text:
Milestones


  
    2020
    
      CS 249r launches at Harvard
      The first offering of the graduate course on TinyML systems. Course notes become the seed of the textbook. The edX TinyML Professional Certificate launches with Google and the tinyML Foundation.
    
  
  
    2021
    
      The TinyML4D network forms
      Partnership with ICTP in Trieste creates the TinyML4D Academic Network. First cohort of 20 universities joins from Latin America, Africa, and Asia. SciTinyML workshop series begins.
    
  
  
    2022
    
      Open-source textbook goes live on GitHub
      The textbook is published as an open repository. Community contributions begin. Second cohort of 20 universities joins TinyML4D. Stars grow as the repo gains visibility.
    
  
  
    2024
    
      Beyond TinyML — Machine Learning Systems
      The textbook expands from TinyML into the full ML systems stack: training, serving, distributed systems, fleet orchestration. Two volumes take shape. mlsysbook.ai launches. IEEE formally cites the work.
    
  
  
    2025
    
      The full curriculum takes shape
      TinyTorch (build-from-scratch ML framework), interactive Marimo labs, hardware kits with Arduino and Seeed, 35 Beamer lecture decks, and a complete instructor hub — the ecosystem goes far beyond the textbook.
    
  
  
    2026
    
      MIT Press publication
      Two-volume hardcover edition forthcoming from MIT Press. 22,800+ GitHub stars. 95+ contributors. 40+ universities across six continents. The open-source edition remains free — always.

title: Contributors
text:
How to Contribute

This curriculum is open source and welcomes contributions of all kinds. Whether you spot a typo, want to improve an explanation, add a figure, or write a new section — your contribution matters.

  
    
    Content
    Fix errors, improve explanations, add examples, or write new sections. Every improvement helps students learn.
  
  
    
    Code & Tooling
    Improve build scripts, add interactive widgets, enhance the website, or contribute to TinyTorch modules.
  
  
    
    Translation
    Help make the curriculum accessible to non-English speakers. Translation efforts are coordinated through GitHub.
  
  
    
    Bug Reports
    Found something wrong? Open an issue on GitHub. Clear bug reports are one of the most valuable contributions.
  



  
     Contribute on GitHub

title: Workshops & Events
text:
Recurring Series
Three recurring programs form the backbone of the community’s learning calendar.


  
    
    SciTinyML Workshop Series
     Annual (typically May–June)
    The annual SciTinyML workshop at ICTP in Trieste brings together researchers and students to apply machine learning to scientific challenges on low-power devices — from environmental monitoring to health diagnostics. Each edition combines foundational TinyML labs with cutting-edge research talks.
    
       Website
       Recordings
    
  

  
    
    TinyML4D Show & Tell
     Monthly (last Thursday)
    A monthly virtual session where students from 20+ countries share TinyML projects — agriculture, healthcare, environmental monitoring, assistive technology. Past recordings cover projects from across Africa, Asia, and Latin America.
    
       Submit a Talk
       Recordings
    
  

  
    
    TinyML4D Seminar Series
     Periodic
    Research seminars featuring domain-specific applications of TinyML at institutions across the developing world — from getting started with Arduino kits to advanced on-device learning techniques.
    
       Recordings

title: Workshops & Events
text:
Free Online Courses
The curriculum extends beyond the textbook through free online courses on edX and Coursera.


  
     edX
    Foundations of TinyML
    The basics of machine learning and embedded systems — your first introduction to the language of TinyML.
  

  
     edX
    Applications of TinyML
    Practical examples: keyword spotting, visual wake words, and gesture recognition on real devices.
  

  
     edX
    Deploying TinyML
    TensorFlow Lite programming for microcontrollers — from model conversion to on-device inference.
  

  
     edX
    MLOps for Scaling TinyML
    Machine Learning Operations through the lens of TinyML — production deployment at the edge.
  

  
     Coursera
    Intro to Embedded ML
    Neural network training and deployment using the Edge Impulse platform.
  

  
     Coursera
    Computer Vision with Embedded ML
    Image classification and object detection using deep learning on edge devices.

      2021–2022

title: Workshops & Events
text:
Watch & Learn
Hundreds of hours of free video content from workshops, seminars, and student presentations.

  
    
    SciTinyML & Show & Tell
    Workshop talks and monthly student project presentations from around the world.
  
  
    
    TinyML4D Seminars
    Research talks on domain-specific TinyML applications from institutions worldwide.
  
  
    
    Discussion Forum
    Ask questions, share projects, and connect with the TinyML education community.

title: Partners & Sponsors
text:
PARTNERS & SPONSORS
  
  From hardware kits in Nairobi to the MIT Press hardcover — none of this happens without partners who believe open education is worth investing in.

title: Partners & Sponsors
text:
Institutional Partners
The academic institutions and organizations that anchor the curriculum and global network.

  

    
      
        
      
      Harvard SEAS
      Home of CS 249r and the ML Systems curriculum.
    

    
      
        
      
      MIT Press
      Publisher of the two-volume hardcover edition.
    

    
      
        
      
      tinyML Foundation
      Non-profit advancing TinyML technology and education worldwide.
    

    
      
        
      
      ICTP
      Co-host of the TinyML4D Academic Network and SciTinyML workshops in Trieste.
    

    
      
        
      
      edX
      Platform for the TinyML Professional Certificate (4 courses).
    

    
      
        
      
      Edge AI Foundation
      Supporting edge AI education and research communities.
    

    
      
        
      
      Harvard Data Science Initiative
      Interdisciplinary data science research and education at Harvard.
    

    
      
        
      
      Harvard Extension School
      Making Harvard courses accessible to a global audience.
    

    
      
        
      
      National Science Foundation
      Funding ML systems research and educational infrastructure.

title: Partners & Sponsors
text:
Industry Sponsors
The companies that provide funding, hardware, and platform access to make this curriculum possible.

  

    
      
        
      
      Google
      Founding sponsor of the edX TinyML series and TensorFlow Lite ecosystem.
    

    
      
        
      
      Arduino
      Hardware partner — every TinyML4D university receives Arduino Tiny ML Kits.
    

    
      
        
      
      Edge Impulse
      Platform partner for embedded ML development and Coursera courses.
    

    
      
        
      
      Seeed Studio
      Hardware partner providing XIAO and Grove ecosystem for deployment labs.

title: Partners & Sponsors
text:
Become a Partner

  
  Support Open AI Education
  $500 ships a hardware kit to a university classroom that needs it. Your sponsorship directly funds hardware for 40+ universities across six continents.
  
    
       Support on Open Collective
    
    
       Contact Us
    
  
  We welcome partnerships in hardware, cloud credits, curriculum co-development, and translation support.
